---
applyTo: "**"
description: "Accessibility guidelines following WCAG 2.2 Level AA"
---

# Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.2 AA)

## Semantic HTML

- Use `<main>`, `<nav>`, `<header>`, `<footer>`, `<article>`, `<section>`
  appropriately.
- Use proper heading hierarchy (`<h1>` → `<h2>` → `<h3>`). Never skip levels.
- Use `<button>` for actions, `<a>` for navigation. Never use `<div>` or
  `<span>` as clickable elements.

## Keyboard Navigation

- All interactive elements must be focusable and operable via keyboard.
- Ensure logical tab order follows visual order.
- Provide visible focus indicators (do not remove `outline`).
- Support `Escape` to close modals/overlays.

## Color and Contrast

- Minimum contrast ratio: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text.
- Never rely on color alone to convey meaning.
- Both light and dark themes must meet contrast requirements.

## Text and Reading

- Text must be resizable up to 200% without loss of content.
- Line height should be at least 1.5x for body text.
- Paragraph spacing should be at least 2x the font size.
- Allow user-configurable font size (Settings page).

## Images and Icons

- Decorative icons: `aria-hidden="true"`.
- Meaningful images: provide descriptive `alt` text.

## Screen Readers

- Use `aria-label` when visible text is insufficient.
- Use `aria-live` regions for dynamic content updates (e.g., search results).
- Ensure search results announce count to screen readers.

## Bible-Specific Considerations

- Verse numbers should be distinguishable but not disruptive to screen readers.
  Consider `<sup>` with appropriate aria treatment.
- Footnote toggles should announce their expanded/collapsed state.
- Chapter headings should be navigable landmarks.
